revision.text:
[[de:Ab (Dekan)]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom of Egypt|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} |TITEL = Ab-(anch-schetu) |NAME = D36:D58 N14-HASH |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Ab
''ˁb''
''Purification'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = Epithet |NAME2 = S34-I2-Z2 |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Anch-schetu
''ˁnḫ-štw''
''Life of the Turtle'' }} '''Ab''' (also '''Ab-anch-schetu''') is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|qid=Q5248738|lt=Decan|1=Decan}}, which comprised at least one {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qid=Q804527}} (decan star) and was part of the {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|qid=Q8928|lt=Constellation|1=Constellation}} {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|en|Cancer (constellation)|qid=Q8849|lt=Cancer|1=Cancer (constellation)}}, whose brightest {{ill|Star|en|Star|qid=Q523|lt=Star|1=Star}} is {{ill|Beta Cancri|en|Beta Cancri|qid=Q13766|lt=Al Tarf|1=Beta Cancri}}. The epithet "Anch-schetu" referred to the {{ill|Turtle|de|Schildkröte|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Turtle|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Turtle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Turtle|qid=Q223044|1=Turtle}}, a sacred yet dangerous creature in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|qid=Q205740|lt=Egyptian mythology|1=Egyptian mythology}}. Associated with the decan Ab, "Ab-anch-schetu," meaning "Purification of the Life of the Turtle," symbolized the death and rebirth of the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|qid=Q3392|lt=Nile|1=Nile}} in connection with the {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|en|Flooding of the Nile|qid=Q1519552|lt=Nile flood|1=Flooding of the Nile}}.Edouard Naville: ''Das aegyptische Todtenbuch der XVIII. bis XX. Dynastie aus verschiedenen Urkunden.'' Akad. Druck- u. Verlagsanstalt, Graz 1971 (Reprint of the 1886 Berlin edition), Spell 161. == Background == Together with the decan {{ill|Schetui|de|Schetui|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schetui|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schetui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schetui|qid=Q2233884|1=Schetui}}, Ab formed the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Ab-Schetui|de|Ab-Schetui|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab-Schetui|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab-Schetui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab-Schetui|qid=Q303840|1=Ab-Schetui}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qid=Q2005559|1=Book of Nut}}, the decan Ab is not explicitly mentioned but is identified under the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|qid=Q165496|1=Nut (goddess)}} as the first decan, which rose {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|heliakisch|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heliacal rising|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heliacal rising|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heliacal rising|qid=Q368373|1=Heliacal rising}} with {{ill|Kenmut|de|Kenmut|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmut|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmut|qid=Q1738771|1=Kenmet}} on the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. The arrangement was based on the reorganization under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|qid=Q19248|lt=Senusret III|1=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) during his seventh year of reign. The decan Ab was always considered in the context of the constellation Ab-Schetui and therefore does not appear by name in the decan lists. In the {{ill|Papyrus Carlsberg 1|de|Papyrus Carlsberg 1|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Carlsberg 1|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Carlsberg 1|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Carlsberg 1|qid=Q25377583|1=Papyrus Carlsberg 1}}, its unique status is commented upon: {{Zitat |Text=The rising of {{ill|Kenmut|de|Kenmut|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmut|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmut|qid=Q1738771|1=Kenmet}} together with Ab-Schetui is the life of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|qid=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}}; that is, the place where Kenmut rises on the "{{ill|Nile flood|de|Hohen Tag|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nile flood|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nile flood|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nile flood|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}" is the place where Ab rises. The place where Schetui rises on the "High Day" is the place where {{ill|Ra (Egyptian mythology)|de|Re|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra (Egyptian mythology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra (Egyptian mythology)|qid=Q20987254|1=RE}} rises. This is the rising of Re; that is: the place of rising that Kenmut makes together with Ab-Schetui. |Autor=Line 15 from Papyrus Carlsberg 1 |ref=Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 52.|qq=}} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|qid=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren.'' Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qid=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385, and supplement volume: ''Das Nutbild.'' == References == {{translated page|de|Ab (Dekan)|version=195395651|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:アブ・アンク・シェトゥイ]] [[de:Ab (Dekan)]] {{wikidata link|Q303855}}
